title: Application automatically goes to background in various scenarios
author: techpavan
body_snippet:
<!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->

## Expected Behavior
<!--- If you're describing a bug, tell us what should happen -->
<!--- If you're suggesting a change/improvement, tell us how it should work -->
While using mRemoteNG, it should behave and remain like a foreground application until the user prefers to minimize this and bring any other application to the foreground.

## Current Behavior
<!--- If describing a bug, tell us what happens instead of the e...

comments.author: federicoculloca
comments.snippet:
I can confirm Scenario 1 and Scenario 2.

I'd add that if I have another application in the foreground and click on mRemoteNG's icon in the taskbar *or* by Alt+Tab the application seems to get focus but if you look at the task bar it doesn't look like it's the active application (the previous application in the foreground looks like it's still the active application.

This is what I see just after clicking the mRemoteNG icon

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/904750/740272...

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
Implemented fork-side fix package for this issue:

- upstream PR: PR #3118 on `mRemoteNG/mRemoteNG`
- change summary:
  - when RDP raises `OnLeaveFullScreenMode`, mRemoteNG now restores main form (if minimized), activates it, and refocuses the active session control
  - reduces cases where mRemoteNG falls behind other windows after leaving fullscreen via RDP bar
- fork CI evidence:
  - CI run 21789549099 on `robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`

If needed, we can add follow-up instrumentation around fullscre...

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available in community release**

This fix is included in a community build from our fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). Available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and let us know if the issue is fully resolved.
